title: Six-Country Comparison Index

subject_profile.summary: Highly educated American, board-certified laboratory animal veterinarian (ACLAM), considering relocation.
stated_values: Rule of law and institutional integrity weighted most heavily
Personal freedom and security from arbitrary state action are foundational
Economic success is contingent on rule of law, not a substitute for it

headline_findings: Sweden and Canada are the two clear positive destinations across all three decisions; effectively tied for the living decision once personal fit is included.
The US and China occupy the bottom two positions for living, reaching them by opposite routes: democratic erosion (US) vs authoritarian baseline (China).
Ukraine outperforms expectations on the long-horizon living decision due to positive outcome skew (a war that must end + EU accession + reconstruction upside), despite high variance.
Mexico outperforms the US on relocation because US deterioration has converged toward Mexico's institutional baseline.
Skew direction is decision-relevant: Ukraine (positive) and China (negative) can have similar central tendencies with opposite implications.
outstanding_work: Full v2 source-discipline re-run, applying the hard exclusion rule uniformly (most urgent for China and US).
Rebuild China economic scores from independent proxies rather than CCP-sourced data.
Apply post-2022 partially-entangled discount to US official data.
Slant-balance audit on all six countries (input sets likely lean unevenly).
Consider reweighting trade/industrial capacity upward (currently under-weights China's manufacturing power).
Re-examine whether Mexico's nearshoring/demographic upside is under-scored under skew discipline.
Optionally run a strict-foundational-weighting variant per subject's stated values (likely drops China below the US).
